data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Simplifying choices improves user decision-making process.
data.technical_spec: Interfaces are streamlined to allow easy content browsing and selection.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: The time to acquire a target is a function of the distance to and size of the target.
data.technical_spec: Content can be readily selected using intuitive UI components like large thumbnails and arrows.

data.title: Consistency and Standards
data.description: Consistency in design and interactions to meet user expectations.
data.technical_spec: Aligns interface changes with standard user practices globally.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Users can hold about 7 (plus or minus 2) pieces of information in their working memory.
data.technical_spec: Home screen limits content categories visible at one time to ease cognitive load.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Users spend most of their time on other sites, so they prefer similar interfaces.
data.technical_spec: Adopts familiar UI elements from similar streaming platforms for ease of use.
